title: Remove admin access requirement for posting blog posts

requirements.text: Remove the admin-only authorization check on the backend createBlogPost (and updateBlogPost if applicable) functions so that any authenticated user can create and publish blog posts without needing admin privileges.

quotes: just remove admin access for posting any blog post

acceptanceCriteria: Any authenticated (non-admin) user can call createBlogPost without receiving an authorization error.
The backend no longer checks isAdmin before allowing post creation or editing.
Admin-only operations unrelated to posting (e.g., deleting posts) remain unchanged.

requirements.text: Remove any frontend admin access guards on the Create and Edit blog post pages (CreateBlogPostPage and EditBlogPostPage) so that any authenticated user can navigate to and submit these pages without being blocked or redirected by an admin check.

quotes: just remove admin access for posting any blog post

acceptanceCriteria: CreateBlogPostPage is accessible to any logged-in user, not just admins.
EditBlogPostPage is accessible to any logged-in user who owns or has permission on the post.
No 'Access Denied' screen or admin-only error message appears for non-admin authenticated users attempting to create or edit posts.
The AdminRoute wrapper (if used for these pages) is replaced with a simple authenticated-user guard or removed entirely.

constraints: Do not remove admin checks from operations that should remain admin-only (e.g., deleting posts, managing users).
Do not modify immutable frontend paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui.
nonGoals: Changing authentication mechanism or Internet Identity integration.
Altering the visual design or layout of any pages.
